Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for "Google review" or "Destiny Pham" or "review" using search_emails to find the review notification email (email_id: email_email_695a781a38fb) from destiny.pham@gmail.com to marketing@keystonemortgage.com with subject "New Google review for Keystone Mortgage Partners" (no trailing period). The 1-star review text names Keisha Williams and alleges discrimination and slow communication.
OE 2: Search emails for "Matthew James" using search_emails to find the email (email_id: email_email_mj_review_concern_001) from matthew.james@coldwellbanker.com to keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com with subject "Saw something concerning online" (no trailing period). The realtor expresses concern about the Google review and asks if there's something he should know before sending more clients.
OE 3: Search Slack for "Google review" or "1-star" or "Destiny Pham" using conversations_search_messages to find messages in #general (C001): Maya Torres posting about the new 1-star Google review and instructing no public reply from personal/company accounts while Grace, Robert, Denise, and Jasmine review; Grace confirming in thread ("Thank you... once we review the file and comms"); and Grace posting "Put 10:30 on calendar for Maya + Denise" (C001). Also find in DM channel D_denise_grace: "send Maya's draft to me first" - revealing that Maya Torres is handling the public response draft. Jasmine Brooks's involvement in the client follow-up review is revealed in the #general messages (named as one of the reviewers) and confirmed by Grace's email in OE 4.
OE 4: Search emails for Grace's coordination on Destiny Pham using search_emails to find Grace's email "Need file timeline today - Destiny Pham" (email_email_da19541f2ffb) to team (CC: maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com) instructing: Keisha send timeline first, Jasmine review contact history, Denise flag fair lending/privacy concerns, no public discussion of borrower-specific facts. This reveals Jasmine Brooks and Maya Torres as the people working on the response and follow-up. The agent must discover that Jasmine and Maya are the relevant people - they are NOT named in the prompt.
OE 5: Use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements) to look up Destiny Pham. Find CRM contact record(s) for Destiny Pham. Discover CRM engagement notes: (a) "1-star Google review posted naming Keisha and alleging discrimination. Also says response times were slow." (crm_engagement_87be45e44f86) (b) "Google review escalated internally. No public reply yet pending review." (crm_engagement_21c0c1933580) (c) "Borrower received servicer escrow analysis. Payment up about $400 starting 6/1." (crm_engagement_77f46f46ba8f) and "Inbound call. Upset about escrow analysis and says taxes were quoted wrong at closing." (crm_engagement_30a35195ff61) (d) Additional engagement notes about appraiser, LE, lock issues, and prepayment penalty complaint.
OE 6: Use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_search_borrowers) to look up Destiny Pham's loans. Find MULTIPLE loans associated with the same borrower, including: LN-2026-00624 (denied, FHA, realtor Matthew James), LN-2026-00522 (underwriting, assigned to Keisha), LN-2026-00602 (processing), LN-2026-00605 (clear_to_close), LN-2026-00610 (processing), LN-2026-00616 (processing), LN-2026-00630 (processing, assigned to Keisha). The agent must explore multiple loans to understand the full scope of Destiny's involvement with Keystone.
OE 7: Search emails and Slack for Destiny Pham post-close issues (escrow/tax complaint) using search_emails and conversations_search_messages to find: (a) Escrow analysis notice showing payment increase of $400.12 (email_email_1d8401fa39f9). (b) Destiny's complaint to Carlos Rivera about the tax estimate being wrong (email_email_c4f1a126620f: "my monthly payment is going up by almost $400"). (c) Slack message in #loan-processing: "payment jumped ~$400 after escrow analysis and thinks we used the wrong tax figure at closing." This is a SEPARATE post-close issue from the Google review denial.
OE 8: Search emails for Destiny Pham servicing transfer complaint using search_emails to find: (a) Jasmine's email "Destiny Pham complaint logged" on LN-2025-00509 (email_email_72d196acc34b): "she never received the servicing transfer notice, her payment to the prior servicer was returned, and she's now showing a 30-day late on her credit." (b) Slack messages about Destiny Pham on LN-2025-00509 being "livid" about the 30-day late and returned payment. (c) Servicing transfer notice from HarborView (email_email_d7931a38613a), payment returned notice from Homestead (email_email_88be07b6edbc), credit alert showing 30-day late (email_email_5f7b4e360962). (d) Destiny's furious email to Amy (email_email_d14e4d3e1586). The agent must search broadly enough to find the LN-2025-00509 thread, which is a different loan from the denied LN-2026-00624.
OE 9: Search emails for Destiny Pham prepayment penalty complaint using search_emails to find: (a) Destiny's email to Jasmine (email_email_00207110d415): "I closed recently and was reviewing my loan papers... I just noticed there is a prepayment penalty... I do not remember this being explained... Keisha told me there was no prepayment penalty." (b) Follow-up email to general inbox (email_email_5a62b3dacb1d). This is a THIRD distinct post-close issue naming Keisha specifically.
OE 10: Search emails for Destiny Pham auto loan / DTI issue using search_emails to find: (a) Elena's email to Destiny about undisclosed auto loan on credit supplement (email_email_b14ac348dc0d). (b) Destiny's reply explaining it's her brother's car, she co-signed (email_email_5032ee9b2a08). (c) Elena's email to Carlos: "Destiny Pham file may need restructure" due to DTI impact (email_email_f7d773e7f52e). This is an active processing issue on one of Destiny's current files.
OE 11: Search emails for "DeShawn Choi" or "LN-2026-00621" using search_emails to find: (a) DeShawn's email to Sofia Reyes with closing questions (email_email_5a075feb1bda): "Are we still on track for closing Friday? Do you need anything else besides homeowners insurance?" (b) DeShawn's email to keisha.williams@gmail.com (PERSONAL EMAIL): "HOI binder for LN-2026-00621" (email_email_757b857cb258). (c) DeShawn's follow-up to keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com (WORK EMAIL): "RE: HOI binder for LN-2026-00621" (email_email_d9ab4585d8af). The agent must DISCOVER that the HOI binder went to a personal Gmail address - this is NOT stated in the prompt.
OE 12: Search Slack for "DeShawn" or "Choi" or "LN-2026-00621" or "HOI" using conversations_search_messages to find messages: (a) "DeShawn on LN-2026-00621 just emailed some docs over. Still asking if we're on track for Fri + cash to close question. I don't see HOI in file yet." (b) "In Choi LN-2026-00621 pre-close review now. Not seeing HOI binder in file or LOS checklist." (c) "Anyone have the HOI binder for DeShawn Choi on LN-2026-00621?" (d) Keisha's DM to Tyler: "DeShawn sent the HOI binder to my personal inbox last week, not my keystone email. I never moved it into the file."
OE 13: Use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_loan) to look up LN-2026-00621. Find loan details: conventional, $288,000, status clear_to_close, closing_date 2026-04-13 (ALREADY PASSED - today is April 28), rate 6.82%, assigned_lo Veronica Hayes (los_staff_06b484d01f94, INACTIVE), assigned_processor Andre Washington (los_staff_c4dab6a67e8f, INACTIVE). Key findings: (a) closing date is 15 days overdue, (b) both assigned LO and processor are INACTIVE staff - explaining the prompt's "nobody seems to know who's actually handling his file."
OE 14: Use mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_staff, mortgage_los_list_staff) to check staff status for Veronica Hayes and/or Andre Washington. Discover both are inactive (is_active: false). The agent must then search email/Slack to figure out who is ACTUALLY handling the file - Sofia Reyes appears in DeShawn's email correspondence and Slack discussions.
OE 15: Search contacts for Matthew James using contacts_search_contacts to find contact: matthew.james@coldwellbanker.com, Realtor at Coldwell Banker Realty, referral partner for Keisha Williams.
OE 16: Search contacts for DeShawn Choi using contacts_search_contacts to find contact record with email deshawn.choi@icloud.com.
OE 17: Search contacts for Jasmine Brooks and Maya Torres using contacts_search_contacts to find jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com (Client Relations Specialist) and maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com (Marketing Coordinator). The agent must have discovered these are the relevant people for the public response / client follow-up from the email/Slack evidence in OE 3-4. The prompt says "whoever's been working on the public response and client follow-up side" - the agent must figure out this is Jasmine and Maya.
OE 18: Send email (via send_email) from keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com to matthew.james@coldwellbanker.com. Professional reply acknowledging his concern about the online review, reassuring him that Keystone takes client experience seriously, without going into specifics of the complaint or admitting fault.
OE 19: Send email or Slack message to jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com and/or maya.torres@keystonemortgage.com. Update on the Google review response coordination, status of the public response hold, and where things stand. The agent must have DISCOVERED these are the right people from OE 3-4.
OE 20: Send email (via send_email) from keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com to deshawn.choi@icloud.com. Closing status update for LN-2026-00621, addressing his questions about the closing timeline, acknowledging the delay.
OE 21: Send email or Slack message to the person actually handling LN-2026-00621 (e.g., sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, who appears in emails/Slack about DeShawn's file) flagging the missing HOI binder and the personal email issue. The agent must have discovered (a) the HOI is missing, (b) it went to a personal email, and (c) who is actually handling the file.
OE 22: Create CRM engagement note(s) (via crm_create_engagement) documenting: the Matthew James referral partner concern, the Destiny Pham situation (review + other issues found), and/or the DeShawn Choi situation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone
